name: Earthjustice
mission: Stay informed on how we hold accountable those who break our environmental laws.
theoryOfChange: The law is the most powerful tool for change. By providing free, top-tier legal representation to public-interest groups, Earthjustice enforces science-driven laws to achieve systemic environmental protections.

scoreSummary: Earthjustice shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Annually recognized by the nation’s chief watchdog groups for charitable organizations for transparency and efficiency

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: 99% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 2.4% of revenue ($2,982,698 on $125,960,142)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.evidence: Niche cause: UNKNOWN (+4); Serves underserved populations (+3)

alignment.rationale: Alignment 27/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.

score_details.score_summary: Earthjustice shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://earthjustice.org)

all_citations.claim: Earthjustice operates programs like The Wild and Healthy Communities and holds 19.3 months of working capital.
The organization reported Total Revenue of $125,960,142 and a program expense ratio of 80.2%.
The organization accepts general donations and voluntary contributions for its environmental legal work.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice maintains robust financial health and transparency, though its specialized focus on legal capacity building results in a higher cost-per-impact ratio compared to direct-aid charities.
areas_for_improvement: High cost per beneficiary due to the resource-intensive nature of legal advocacy
Lower alignment for donors seeking direct service or traditional humanitarian aid

dimension_explanations.alignment: As an advocacy-focused organization, Earthjustice addresses high-urgency environmental causes, though it represents a low donor fit for those specifically seeking Islamic-centered humanitarian projects [1]. Contributions are processed as general charitable giving or sadaqah [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a commitment to its mission by directing a program expense ratio of 80.2% toward its legal programs [2]. While it maintains a significant 19.3 months of working capital, the impact is categorized as capacity building, which involves long-term advocacy rather than immediate direct service [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: Earthjustice serves as a premier nonprofit public interest law firm dedicated to wielding the power of partnership and the law to protect the environment.
strengths: High operational efficiency with a program expense ratio of 80.2%
Strong financial stability with 19.3 months of working capital
Highly efficient fundraising cost of only $0.12 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: Founded as a legal advocate for the natural world, Earthjustice operates major programs including The Wild and Healthy Communities to secure environmental protections [1]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a Total Revenue of $125,960,142 to support its litigation and advocacy efforts [2].

all_citations.quote: Your gift is a tax-deductible contribution.

all_citations.claim: over 100 million views across its platforms

all_citations.quote: Areas Served: North America, South America, United States, South Africa

all_citations.claim: Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: Earthjustice maintains robust financial health and transparency, though its specialized focus on legal capacity building results in a higher cost-per-impact ratio compared to direct-aid charities.

areas_for_improvement.context: Due to the resource-intensive nature of litigation, the estimated cost per beneficiary is <cite id="6">$118,249.37</cite>, which may be high for donors prioritizing direct service.
Despite international programs, approximately <cite id="6">80.2% of expenses remain within the US</cite>, limiting its impact in the most vulnerable global regions.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization maintains high transparency with audited financials and a 100% independent board.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization mitigates these by focusing on 'precedent-setting' cases that have broad, systemic impacts beyond a single beneficiary.
risk_factors: <cite id="6">High domestic burn rate</cite> relative to international needs
<cite id="6">High cost per beneficiary</cite> inherent to legal models
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve the high cost of legal delivery and a heavy domestic focus. <cite id="6">99% of spending is domestic</cite>, which may not align with donors seeking global humanitarian impact.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023, outcomes from 2025 report
data_gaps: No Charity Navigator overall rating score
confidence.notes: Strong financial and outcome data available.

known_gaps: Not fully rated by Charity Navigator
High cost per beneficiary due to legal model

alignment.explanation: Alignment is moderate as the charity focuses on <cite id="6">advocacy and legal work</cite> rather than direct service. While it serves underserved populations, it is <cite id="3">Sadaqah-eligible only</cite>.
alignment.improvement: Alignment for Muslim donors could be strengthened by <cite id="6">clearly stating the impact of its work on Muslim-majority communities</cite> affected by climate change.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust Earthjustice due to its <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> and its <cite id="7">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>. The organization has a <cite id="1">55-year track record</cite> of successful litigation and maintains <cite id="2">audited financial statements</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could improve its governance score by addressing the <cite id="6">CEO compensation ratio</cite>, which currently warrants scrutiny for organizations of this scale.

impact.explanation: Impact is delivered through capacity building and systemic change, with <cite id="1">79.9% of funds</cite> reaching programs. The organization tracks outcomes such as <cite id="4">37,075 students using its curriculum</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement could be improved by <cite id="6">documenting specific triggers for the deployment of its high reserves</cite> to ensure capital is being used effectively.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade A - Verified outcomes and documented theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds partner organizations to strengthen environmental movements and community-led litigation.

rich_narrative.headline: Earthjustice serves as a premier nonprofit public interest law firm dedicated to wielding the power of partnership and the law to protect the environment.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize long-term systemic change through the legal system and value environmental protection as a form of sadaqah jariyah.
donor_motivations: Want to use the law to protect the environment and public health
Value highly stable, established institutions with deep expertise
Prefer funding systemic advocacy over individual direct aid
Interested in tribal sovereignty and climate justice
giving_considerations: Donations support long-term litigation that may take years to resolve
The organization is extremely well-funded with high reserves
Impact is measured in legal victories and policy shifts rather than individual aid packages
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, direct humanitarian relief like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A reflects a verified theory of change and clear tracking of outcome metrics like curriculum reach and legislative analysis.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Earthjustice uses the power of the law to preserve wild lands, build biodiversity, and protect communities from climate change through precedent-setting litigation.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: In legal advocacy, evidence of success is found in court rulings and policy changes that protect millions of people simultaneously.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While Earthjustice has high reserves, the scale of climate litigation requires significant and sustained capital to match well-funded corporate opponents.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="1">19.3 months of working capital</cite>, which is significantly higher than many peers in the advocacy space.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Broader focus on policy advocacy alongside litigation.
similar_organizations.name: Natural Resources Defense Council

similar_organizations.differentiator: Stronger focus on grassroots mobilization and local chapters.
similar_organizations.name: Sierra Club Foundation

similar_organizations.differentiator: A major grantmaking partner for Earthjustice's domestic work.
similar_organizations.name: Tides Foundation

strengths.detail: Earthjustice maintains a <cite id="1">80.2% program expense ratio</cite>, ensuring the vast majority of funds support its environmental litigation and advocacy work.
The organization demonstrates exceptional fundraising efficiency, spending only <cite id="1">$0.12 to raise every $1</cite> in revenue.
With <cite id="1">19.3 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, the charity is well-positioned to sustain long-term legal battles that can span several years.

strengths_deep_dive: Earthjustice's strength lies in its institutional maturity and specialized delivery model. As a legal advocate, it leverages <cite id="2">$294.7 million in total assets</cite> to provide high-level legal representation for environmental causes that would otherwise lack the resources to challenge powerful interests. Its governance is supported by a <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> of 33 members, ensuring objective oversight of its <cite id="2">$148 million in annual expenses</cite>. Furthermore, the organization has demonstrated strategic growth with a <cite id="2">3-year revenue CAGR of 4.7%</cite>, reflecting steady donor trust and financial resilience.
rich_narrative.summary: Earthjustice is a premier nonprofit public interest law firm dedicated to protecting the environment through legal action and strategic partnerships. <cite id="1">founded in 1971</cite>, the organization has evolved into a leading advocate for the natural world, focusing on five core program areas: Climate and Energy, Healthy Communities, Lands and Wildlife, Sustainable Food and Farming, and Tribal Sovereignty Support. In the 2023 fiscal year, Earthjustice reported <cite id="2">Total Revenue of $125,960,142</cite>, primarily driven by <cite id="2">$117.4 million in public contributions</cite>. The organization maintains a lean operational structure, directing <cite id="1">79.9% of its budget toward program expenses</cite>, which supports its extensive litigation docket. Beyond its legal work, Earthjustice engages in broad public outreach, achieving <cite id="4">over 100 million views across its platforms</cite> to raise awareness for environmental justice. While the organization operates primarily within the United States, it also extends its reach to <cite id="5">South America and South Africa</cite> to address global climate challenges. For donors seeking to support systemic environmental change through the judicial system, Earthjustice offers a stable and highly specialized platform for sadaqah giving.
